//: E1-T26 — layer over sail_riscv_sim's default config so the SAIL reference matches our DUT's ISA (rv64gc + S/U + Sv39/Sv48). Applied via `--config-override` by riscof_sail.py. Only the deltas from the default config are listed.

extensions.//Svnapot: our DUT treats PTE bit 63 (N) as reserved and page-faults it (E1-T20); disable so Sail does too (fixes vm_*_reserved_svnapot).
extensions.//Svpbmt: our DUT treats PTE bits 62:61 (PBMT) / [60:54] as reserved and page-faults them (E1-T20); disable so Sail does too (fixes vm_*_pte_reserved_field).
extensions.//Sv57: E1-T28: our DUT now IMPLEMENTS Sv57 (5-level paging, satp MODE=10) — leave it ENABLED in the reference (Sail default) so the vm_sv57 tests genuinely run the 5-level walk against BOTH.
extensions.//Svrsw60t59b: our DUT treats PTE bits [63:54] (incl. 60:59) as reserved and page-faults them (E1-T20, PTE_RESERVED_HI); disable this reserved-for-SW extension so Sail faults 60:59 too (fixes vm_*_pte_reserved_field).

memory.//pmp: E1-T27/T28: our DUT implements 64 PMP entries — match the reference so the pmpm_all_entries_check 64-region tests genuinely run 64 entries on BOTH (Sail default is 16). BOTH count AND usable_count must be 64: Sail treats entries above usable_count as read-only-zero, so leaving usable_count at its default 16 made pmpaddr16..63/pmpcfg legalize to 0 and Sail allowed M-mode stores our (correct) DUT trapped — the pmpm-01/02/03 divergence.

misaligned.//: Scalar misaligned already proceeds (default) — matches our DUT. Atomics require alignment: our DUT raises *AddrMisaligned (cause 4/6 = AlignmentException), not Sail's default AccessFault.
